title: JIT IR translation — tuple and list operations
purpose: Tuple and list construction are the most common heap-allocating operations in BEAM code. Every Gleam function that returns a tagged union ({ok, Value} or {error, Reason}) constructs a tuple. List processing (map, filter, fold) constructs cons cells in a loop. JIT-compiling these to native allocation calls with proper GC safepoints avoids the interpreter dispatch overhead for every allocation. This brief uses the runtime allocation helpers from B-134 (jit_alloc_tuple, jit_alloc_cons) and the safepoint infrastructure from B-134.
task: Implement IR translation for tuple and list construction/access opcodes in crates/beamr/src/jit/ir_terms.rs. put_tuple2 emits a jit_alloc_tuple call followed by element writes. put_list emits a jit_alloc_cons call. get_tuple_element, get_list, get_hd, get_tl compile to direct memory loads from the heap. All construction opcodes emit a GC safepoint (via B-134's SafepointEmitter) before the allocation call.

requirements.title: Tuple construction IR — put_tuple2
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL translate put_tuple2 into Cranelift IR that: (1) emits a GC safepoint with all live Term values in the root set, (2) calls jit_alloc_tuple(process, arity) via the RuntimeHelpers function pointer, (3) writes each element to the allocated tuple at the correct offset (header + element_index * word_size). THE SYSTEM SHALL tag the resulting pointer as a boxed Term before storing it in the destination register.
acceptance: translate_put_tuple2(builder, emitter, dest, elements) emits safepoint + alloc + element writes
The safepoint root set includes all elements being written (they are live across the allocation)
Element writes use Cranelift store instructions at header_ptr + (i+1) * 8
The destination register receives a tagged boxed Term (raw pointer | BOXED_TAG)
Test: compiled put_tuple2 with elements [atom_ok, small_int(42)] -> valid {ok, 42} tuple
Test: compiled put_tuple2 with arity 5 -> all 5 elements accessible via get_tuple_element

requirements.title: Tuple element access IR — get_tuple_element
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL translate get_tuple_element into Cranelift IR that: (1) untags the boxed pointer (value AND NOT TAG_MASK), (2) loads the element at offset (element_index + 1) * word_size from the untagged pointer. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT emit a GC safepoint for read-only access (no allocation). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT check the tuple arity — the BEAM compiler guarantees the index is in bounds after a successful test_arity guard.
acceptance: translate_get_tuple_element(builder, tuple, index, dest) emits untag + load
No safepoint emitted (read-only operation)
Load offset is (index + 1) * 8 (skip tuple header word)
Test: construct tuple {a, b, c}, get_tuple_element index 1 -> returns b
Test: get_tuple_element index 0 on {ok, 42} -> returns atom ok

requirements.title: List construction IR — put_list
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL translate put_list into Cranelift IR that: (1) emits a GC safepoint with head, tail, and all other live Terms in the root set, (2) calls jit_alloc_cons(process, head, tail) via the RuntimeHelpers function pointer, (3) stores the returned tagged list Term in the destination register. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT construct the cons cell with separate alloc + store — jit_alloc_cons handles both allocation and initialization atomically.
acceptance: translate_put_list(builder, emitter, head, tail, dest) emits safepoint + alloc_cons call
The safepoint root set includes head and tail (live across allocation)
The destination register receives the tagged list Term from jit_alloc_cons
Test: compiled put_list with head=1, tail=[] -> valid [1] list
Test: compiled chain of 3 put_list calls -> valid [1, 2, 3] list

requirements.title: List destructuring IR — get_list, get_hd, get_tl
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL translate get_list into Cranelift IR that: (1) untags the list pointer (value AND NOT TAG_MASK), (2) loads the head from offset 0, (3) loads the tail from offset word_size. THE SYSTEM SHALL translate get_hd and get_tl as single loads from offset 0 and word_size respectively. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT emit a GC safepoint for read-only access.
acceptance: translate_get_list(builder, list, dest_hd, dest_tl) emits untag + two loads
translate_get_hd(builder, list, dest) emits untag + load at offset 0
translate_get_tl(builder, list, dest) emits untag + load at offset 8
No safepoints emitted (read-only operations)
Test: construct [1, 2, 3], get_hd -> 1, get_tl -> [2, 3]
Test: get_list on [a | b] -> hd=a, tl=b

requirements.title: Nested construction with correct safepoint root tracking
requirements.spec: WHEN constructing nested structures (e.g. {ok, [1, 2, 3]} which requires put_list * 3 + put_tuple2), THE SYSTEM SHALL correctly track intermediate values in the safepoint root set. Each allocation may trigger GC which may relocate earlier allocations. THE SYSTEM SHALL ensure the SafepointEmitter tracks the intermediate tagged Term values (cons cells, partial tuples) so GC can relocate them if needed.
acceptance: Intermediate cons cell Terms are added to the root set before the next allocation
After each allocation, the result is tracked in the root set for subsequent allocations
Intermediate values are untracked when no longer needed (dead after final use)
Test: compiled {ok, [1, 2]} with minimum heap size process -> correct tuple with correct list (GC forced between allocations verifies root tracking)
Test: compiled [[1, 2], [3, 4]] with minimum heap size process -> correct nested list structure

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement map construction in JIT — map operations deoptimise to the interpreter
SHALL NOT implement binary construction in JIT — binary operations deoptimise to the interpreter
SHALL NOT implement init/0 (tuple initialisation) separately — put_tuple2 handles full initialisation
SHALL NOT check tuple arity on get_tuple_element — BEAM compiler guarantees correctness via prior guard
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
ir_terms.rs is under 500 lines of production code
Every put_tuple2 and put_list translation emits a GC safepoint before the allocation call
No get_tuple_element or get_list/get_hd/get_tl translation emits a safepoint
